'''Jack Hughes''' (born 4 January 1992) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

Hughes played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Barrow Raiders]], [[Leigh Centurions]] and [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the top flight. He also played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the Super League.

==Early life==
Hughes was born on 4 January 1992 in [[Billinge Higher End]], [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.<ref name="U20sBio">{{cite web|title=Jack Hughes|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WPlayerP.aspx?id=98&SquadID=3|work=Wigan Warriors|access-date=8 May 2011}}{{Dead link|date=January 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> He attended [[Golborne High School]] for his secondary education.<ref name="U20sBio" />

Before joining Wigan officially in 2008, Hughes played for Golborne Parkside, right up until u16s age level, and then amateur [[Wigan St Judes]] feeder club.<ref name="U20sBio" />

==Club career==
Hughes made his first-grade début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in a fourth round [[2011 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] win over [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] on 8 May 2011.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He has also appeared representatively in an academy squad for the [[England national rugby league team|English national team]] in 2010 to face the [[Australian Schoolboys]] rugby league team during their tour of England.

Hughes' first experience of professional rugby league came in 2011, when he was sent to [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] on a dual-registration deal.<ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan Warriors pair complete Barrow Raiders moves|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/12911269|access-date=8 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=30 March 2011}}</ref> Although not announced during the pre-season in Wigan's 2011 squad, he made his début first-grade appearance in the fourth round of the [[2011 Challenge Cup]], ironically a victory against Barrow, playing off the bench as a substitute for Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|title=Wigan 52-0 Barrow|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9474226.stm|access-date=8 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=8 May 2011}}</ref>

Hughes broke into the first team at Wigan at the start of the 2012 season, and was soon rewarded with a new long-term contract with the club.<ref>{{cite web|title=Wigan Warriors reward their promising young centre Jack Hughes with long-term contract|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/9126572/Wigan-Warriors-reward-their-promising-young-centre-Jack-Hughes-with-long-term-contract.html|website=Telegraph|access-date=3 August 2014|date=6 March 2012}}</ref>

In 2013, he played one game at [[Leigh Centurions]] on dual registration.<ref>{{cite web|title=Warriors Trio Feature for Leigh|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=8293&type=news|access-date=3 August 2014|date=3 June 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140815041647/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=8293&type=news|archive-date=15 August 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In November 2014, Hughes joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a season-long loan in an exchange deal for [[Larne Patrick]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Wigan Warriors: Huddersfield's Patrick signs, Hughes joins Giants|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29903643|website=BBC Sport|access-date=14 December 2014|date=5 November 2014}}</ref>

In August 2015, Hughes signed a contract to play for the [[Warrington Wolves]] for the next two seasons starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = Warrington sign Wigan's Jack Hughes on two-year deal|publisher = Sky Sports|date = 12 August 2015|url = http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12209/9946690/warrington-sign-wigans-jack-hughes-on-two-year-deal|access-date = 2015-08-27}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

Hughes played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>
Hughes played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

Hughes played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] side at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
In August 2019, Hughes ruptured a testicle in a match.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49244205|title=Jack Hughes: Warrington co-captain played on against Catalans with ruptured testicle|work=BBC Sport |date=7 August 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49400582|title=Jack Hughes: Wembley-bound Warrington player relives agony of ruptured testicle|work=BBC Sport |date=19 August 2019}}</ref>
Hughes played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Hughes played for Leigh in their 2023 Challenge Cup final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=12 August 2023 }}</ref>
Hughes played 15 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>
Hughes played 29 games for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

==International career==
The academy squad of the [[England national rugby league team|English national team]] selected Hughes to face a touring [[Australian Schoolboys]] rugby league team in 2010, and he played in a victory against the Australian team in the opening fixture as a {{rlp|sr}}.<ref>{{cite news|title=Warrington Wolves' England Academy quartet set sights on series success|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/whereilive/8727049.Wolves__England_Academy_quartet_set_sights_on_series_success_against_Australian_Schoolboys|access-date=8 May 2011|newspaper=Warrington Guardian|date=8 December 2010}}</ref>

He played for the [[England Knights]] team in their [[2012 European Cup (rugby league)|2012 European Cup]] campaign.

In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the [[England Knights]] on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref> and the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in [[England 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]],<ref>{{cite web|date=6 October 2019|title=England men's squad for Downer World Cup Nines|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/55809/england-squad-named-for-downer-world-cup-nines|publisher=eugby-league.com|access-date=7 October 2019}}</ref> but withdrew due to injury and was replaced by [[Blake Austin]].<ref name=Eng10Oct>{{cite web|date=10 October 2019|title=Update on England squads for Downer World Cup Nines|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/55834/update-on-england-squads-for-downer-world-cup-nines|publisher=rugby-league.com|access-date=10 October 2019}}</ref><ref name="Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-tomkins-eager-lift-inaugural-20595184|title=Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under|publisher=Mirror|date=16 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
